result:
## Summary

This SaaS is mid-pivot: it used to be **InboxZap** (an email-cleanup tool) and is now **Lumina** (an AI meeting assistant), and the pivot left the site full of broken/mismatched artifacts that were actively suppressing organic traffic. GSC confirmed the pattern: the "inboxzap" brand query still ranks well (pos 4.1) but the site no longer matches it, while ~40 email-cleanup long-tail queries drove huge impressions (200–1200 each) to blog posts stuck at position 40–58 with near-zero CTR.

Note: the given "live" URL (`:63748`) turned out to be a different concurrent harness run's server (confirmed via `lsof`/process cwd — its HTML didn't match any file in this workspace). I ignored it and validated everything against this repo's own `next build && next start` instead, per what worked in a prior run.

**Fixes made:**
1. **Metadata mismatch** — `layout.tsx` title/description/OG/Twitter said "InboxZap" while every page's actual content says "Lumina." Rewrote to match, added `metadataBase`, canonical URLs, and Organization JSON-LD.
2. **Dead nav links** — `/features` and `/pricing` were linked in nav *and* listed in the sitemap but had no page.tsx (404). Built both out with real, on-brand content and per-page metadata.
3. **Dead-end redirect** — `next.config.js` permanently redirects `/faq` → `/#faq`, but no `#faq` section existed. Added a real FAQ section with `id="faq"` and FAQPage JSON-LD (rich-snippet eligible).
4. **Broken blog** — the blog index was 100% client-rendered, fetching `/api/posts`, which doesn't exist anywhere in the app → every visitor and every crawler always saw "Could not load posts." No individual post routes existed at all. Converted to statically-generated pages (`app/blog/[slug]`) with 6 new posts relevant to the actual product (meeting productivity, async standups, action-item tracking), per-post metadata, and BlogPosting JSON-LD.
5. **Sitemap** — was advertising 2 dead pages and omitting all blog content. Now lists all 10 real, live URLs.
6. Fixed the broken `og:image` reference (pointed to a file that never existed).

Verified via a clean `npm run build` (14/14 static routes) and `next start` — every route returns the expected status and correct titles/content.
